prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes that stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. For CSCO, an annualized volatility of 22% was assumed, reflecting its typical historical behavior as a large-cap tech stock. The drift (expected return) was set to 0% for this short 18-day period, as volatility typically dominates over such a short timeframe. The point estimate represents the median of the projected log-normal price distribution, and the confidence intervals are derived from the calculated standard deviation of the logarithmic returns over the 18-day period.
